LHX.sentence: L3Harris ticked up nicely, though the real thing to watch is their profit margins, which are running lower than usual for a defense company and hold the key to the story here.

ASML.sentence: ASML slipped a little, but the business hasn't changed — they're still the only company that makes a certain kind of chipmaking machine, and demand keeps building.

LIN.sentence: Linde is quietly doing what it always does — supplying industrial gases under long contracts and nudging prices up — but the stock is near the top of its range, so it's priced about as richly as it deserves.

MSFT.sentence: No fresh data on Microsoft today, so nothing to report — the AI cloud story just keeps grinding along quietly.

TMO.sentence: Thermo Fisher barely moved, but its lab equipment sales are at a low point in their cycle and the stock looks inexpensive relative to how it usually trades, which often precedes a recovery.

ENB.sentence: Enbridge is steady — its pipelines earn toll-road-style income and pay a solid dividend, though that dividend is a bit less generous than usual compared to safe bonds these days.

NOW.sentence: No data on ServiceNow today, so there's nothing new to say — their AI workflow tools for big companies and the government keep chugging along on long-term contracts.

GLNCY.sentence: Glencore's mining profits are at a deep low point right now, which for a commodities company has historically been the moment things start to turn back up.

KOF.sentence: Coca-Cola FEMSA is uneventful — most of its sales are in Mexican pesos, and the peso has been stable, so there's nothing shaking the business either way.

PBR.A.sentence: Petrobras is paying a hefty dividend that oil prices can currently support, though as always the wildcard is whether Brazil's government decides to keep those payouts flowing.

AMKBY.sentence: Maersk, the container-shipping giant, had a nice up day and is trading below the value of its own ships, which is about as cheap as this kind of business tends to get.
